message:
My 8-year-old insisted our smoke alarm was "too loud" and asked if we could turn it down. I told her it's supposed to be loud—that's literally the job—and she looked at me like I'd suggested we keep a dragon in the garage.

But it got me thinking: she's never actually *heard* one go off in a real moment. Kids (and honestly, some of us) don't always connect the "boring safety thing" with why it actually matters until it feels real.

So we walked through a super simple plan together: where we'd go if there was a fire, where we'd meet outside, that whole thing. She took it seriously once she understood it wasn't just a random rule.

Turned out teaching her was the best reminder *I* needed too. When's the last time you and your people talked through yours? This little refresher actually made it click for us.
